perturbation.original_text: A. CONSULTANT'S "ENDORSEMENT" means the right to use the CONSULTANT'S name, fame, nickname, autograph, voice, facsimile, signature, photograph, likeness, and image in connection with the marketing, advertising, promotion and sale of ADAMS GOLF'S PRODUCT.
perturbation.changed_text: A. CONSULTANT'S "ENDORSEMENT" means the right to use the CONSULTANT'S name solely, and does not include fame, nickname, autograph, voice, facsimile, signature, photograph, likeness, and image in connection with the marketing, advertising, promotion and sale of ADAMS GOLF'S PRODUCT.
perturbation.explanation: This change restricts the definition of 'ENDORSEMENT' to only the CONSULTANT'S name. This contradicts later sections that refer to likeness and other aspects as part of the endorsement, specifically in Section 4, where it discusses CONSULTANT's name and/or likeness.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding paragraphs 4A, 4B and 4C above, CONSULTANT shall be entitled to endorse and play the [*****]. The parties expressly agree that CONSULTANT may permit [*****] the use of CONSULTANT'S name and/or likeness in [*****] print and/or television advertisement provided that this is executed in a manner consistent with [*****] past [*****] advertising practice using similarly situated professional golfers with competing golf club endorsement agreements that include [*****]. If [*****] use of CONSULTANT varies from past practice of utilizing similarly situated professionals, CONSULTANT will take action to remedy the situation [*****]. If CONSULTANT participates directly in any [*****] sponsored photo shoots for advertising or PR, CONSULTANT shall wear his ADAMS GOLF [*****]. The parties further expressly agree that CONSULTANT'S endorsement of the [*****] shall not include the right to place a [*****] logo on his ADAMS GOLF [*****] or the ADAMS GOLF [*****]. If CONSULTANT'S relationship with [*****] terminates during this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall be permitted to replace the [*****] endorsement with another [*****] endorsement under the same terms and conditions expressed herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'ENDORSEMENT' to solely the CONSULTANT'S name. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which references 'CONSULTANT'S name and/or likeness' in the context of endorsements. The original definition included likeness, and by removing it, the agreement becomes inconsistent, because section 4 later refers to name AND likeness for endorsement. This is a clear in-text contradiction under 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction', because the meaning of 'ENDORSEMENT' is defined inconsistently in different parts of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 5. EXCLUSIVE USE OF PRODUCT During the term of this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall exclusively play/use the MANDATORY PRODUCT. (It is expressly understood by the parties that CONSULTANT may play [* ****] clubs in the bag other than ADAMS GOLF clubs including, but not limited to, a putter by a manufacturer other than ADAMS GOLF but may not endorse those clubs and/or putter.)
perturbation.changed_text: 5. EXCLUSIVE USE OF PRODUCT During the term of this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall exclusively play/use the MANDATORY PRODUCT. Furthermore CONSULTANT can make usage of any kind of equipment on the bag other than ADAMS GOLF.
perturbation.explanation: Here, we remove the restriction that the player may not endorse clubs from other manufacturers, creating a contradiction with section 6, where it specifies conditions under which the consultant promotes a non-competitive product.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If CONSULTANT endorses or promotes a non-competitive product and in that endorsement or promotion CONSULTANT wears, plays, uses, holds or is in any way associated with a product that would constitute PRODUCT as defined under this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall use objectively reasonable best efforts to ensure that PRODUCT is an ADAMS GOLF PRODUCT and it shall not be altered or changed in appearance in the endorsement in any manner whatsoever without the express written consent of ADAMS GOLF. When endorsing a non-competitive product, under no circumstances shall CONSULTANT wear, play, use, hold or in any way be associated with an ADAMS GOLF competitor's Product.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5 placed a restriction on the consultant endorsing clubs from other manufacturers, while the contradicted text (regarding non-competitive products) allows for endorsements under specific conditions. The changed text in Section 5 removes this endorsement restriction altogether. This creates a clear contradiction, as Section 5 now permits the consultant to use any equipment without endorsement restrictions, while the 'Contradicted Text' (Section 6) outlines specific conditions for endorsement of non-competitive products, which now conflict with the lack of restrictions imposed by section 5.

perturbation.original_text: A. During the term of this Agreement, ADAMS GOLF shall provide CONSULTANT with sufficient quantities of such MANDATORY PRODUCTS for CONSULTANT'S use as CONSULTANT may reasonably need to fulfill his obligations under this agreement. ADAMS GOLF shall pay all charges in connection with the delivery of MANDATORY PRODUCTS to CONSULTANT.
perturbation.changed_text: A. During the term of this Agreement, ADAMS GOLF shall make a good faith effort to provide CONSULTANT with MANDATORY PRODUCTS.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the obligation to provide MANDATORY PRODUCTS from 'shall provide' to 'make a good faith effort to provide.' This weakens the guarantee of product supply, potentially contradicting Section 10, which states CONSULTANT shall use best efforts to demonstrate, discuss and emphasize the newest features of ADAMS GOLF PRODUCT. By weakening the supply promise, the Consultant may not have any product available, failing his obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall use best efforts to wear the headwear and display ADAMS GOLF'S brand name and to demonstrate, discuss and emphasize the newest features of ADAMS GOLF PRODUCT at every opportunity including but not limited to all Senior PGA tour events and promotional and advertising events in which CONSULTANT takes part.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates ADAMS GOLF to 'provide CONSULTANT with sufficient quantities of such MANDATORY PRODUCTS'. The changed text weakens this obligation to a 'good faith effort to provide CONSULTANT with MANDATORY PRODUCTS.' This directly contradicts the obligation in the 'Contradicted Text' which requires the CONSULTANT to 'demonstrate, discuss and emphasize the newest features of ADAMS GOLF PRODUCT'. If ADAMS GOLF only makes a 'good faith effort' and doesn't actually provide the product, the CONSULTANT cannot fulfill his obligation, creating a clear contradiction and making enforcement of the Consultant's obligations uncertain.
